title: CC narrative review S07 panel a1

payload.notes: S07 delivers its B06 slice exactly: caption matches the page narration's first clause ("within her first hour asleep... eight GPUs") nearly verbatim; all expected_literals (8/8, RUNNING, T-18:30) are baked and legible, giving the launch moment its hard evidence anchor — the GPU LAUNCH CONSOLE technical figure IS the room's centerpiece, as scripted. NARRATIVE-BEAT FIDELITY (5): S07's job is the LAUNCH-only slice of B06 (S07 launch → S08 curve lift → S09 silent bottom panel) and it stays in its lane — no 0.60→0.71 curve leak, no provisional-evidence payoff pulled forward; the role split is exactly the scripted one: the blue executor mid-leap slamming the LAUNCH key with his shout ('上 GPU！八张全开！') while the green reviewer stands behind, arms crossed, eyes already on the ALLOC LOG STREAM ('日志我盯着。') — exuberance vs vigilance, staging the beat's 'every point experiment-bridge streams back is provisional' tension without saying it; the bounce contract holds (a HIGH launch jump but clearly below his hand-off MAX, preserving the bounce ladder for later beats); the 2x4 all-green GPU grid with the big 8/8 counter + green RUNNING chip and the amber DDL T-18:30 reads as 'her first hour asleep, already running on eight cards' in one glance; the warm Edison lamp and '</>' mug breaking the cool #0A0E27 void keep the two-world palette grammar intact. COMPOSITION (STORY) 4/5: reading order executor-shout → LAUNCH spark → 8/8 + RUNNING → reviewer → log stream lands the beat in one read; the half-step deduction is that the right-side ALLOC LOG STREAM panel (eight ALLOCATED lines + ALL GPUS READY.) is rich enough to briefly compete with the central 8/8 + RUNNING anchor on first read — structural, not beat-level. NARRATIVE VERDICT: PASS on story (no beat-level rejection). Visual identity-lock / artifact severity / safezone / baked-text quality are NOT my call — left to the visual reviewers (Gemini + Codex).
